results.rendered_html: 1. īrma (padap. : īrmā) adv. auf der Stelle, hier, hierher  NIR. 5,25 . īrmā puraṃdhirajahādarātīḥ  ṚV. 4,27,2 . īrmā tasthuṣīrahabhirduduhre 5,62,2 . īrmānyadvapuṣe vapuścakraṃ rathasya yemathuḥ 73,3 . yuvo rathasya pari cakramīyata īrmānyadvāmiṣaṇyati 8,22,4 . īrmaiva te nyaviśanta kepayaḥ 10,44,6 .

results.rendered_html:
2. īrma 1〉 m. Arm, Vorderschenkel eines Thieres: īrmābhyāmayanaṃ jātaṃ sakthibhyāṃ ca vaśe tava  AV. 10,10,21 . asau cāsau cermau  ŚAT. BR. 10,6,5,3 .  NIR. 5,25 .
— 2〉 n. Wunde  Uṇ. 1,143 .  AK. 2,6,2,5 .  H. 465  (nach dem Sch. auch m.). m.  HĀR. 156 . — In der ersten Bed. von īr (womit das Thier ausgreift); īrma Wunde ist mit arus zusammenzustellen.

results.rendered_html:
īrma a. [īr-mak]
1 Agitated.
2 Going constantly or instigating everything ( Sāy. )
— ind. Here, in or to this place.
— rmaḥ The arm; the fore-quarter of an animal.
— rmaṃ A wound, sore (m. also).
